stemHtml: The crew of a swept-wing aircraft are performing slow flight, when they enter a stall at a low altitude, but with enough height to recover safely. To recover from the stall in this aircraft, the crew should…

options.a: Immediately apply full but avoid any pitch-up to keep the aircraft from entering a deep stall.
options.b: Pitch down more than usual to counter the nose-up tendency.
options.c: Keep thrust at idle and let the aircraft stabilise.
options.d: Bank with ailerons to prevent a wing drop.
